name: SAP Business Technology Platform API
what_it_does: SAP's OData and REST APIs for accessing enterprise business data including finance, procurement, supply chain, HR, and customer management across SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP.
use_cases: Querying financial data (invoices, POs, GL accounts) from SAP systems
Integrating SAP procurement workflows into external approval systems
Pulling SAP supply chain and inventory data for analytics
Automating employee data queries from SAP HCM
Building custom SAP integration middleware via API Hub
not_for: Teams without SAP systems or licenses
Small/medium businesses (SAP is enterprise-only in practice)
Rapid development without SAP integration expertise
Simple data storage without ERP requirements
best_when: Your organization runs SAP and you need to integrate SAP business data with external tools, agents, or custom applications.
avoid_when: You don't use SAP, or need a modern developer-friendly ERP API.

auth.notes: OAuth2 via SAP BTP XSUAA service. Basic auth for legacy on-premise systems. SAP's auth flows are complex and system-configuration-dependent. API keys via SAP API Hub for sandbox.

pricing.free_tier_limits: SAP BTP free tier and sandbox on api.sap.com for testing
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise licensing — SAP S/4HANA licenses from hundreds of thousands per year
pricing.notes: Enterprise licensing is extremely expensive. API access is included with SAP licenses. Sandbox testing available on api.sap.com without purchase.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by SAP deployment and licensing tier

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OData errors follow standard format but SAP-specific error messages are often cryptic and require SAP expertise to decode. Error codes reference SAP message classes.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: OData UPSERT operations available. Standard POST creates are not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: SAP uses OData (v2 or v4) not standard REST — agents must understand OData query syntax ($filter, $expand, etc.)
CSRF tokens required for write operations in OData v2 — must fetch token before POST/PUT/DELETE
SAP landscape typically has multiple systems (DEV, QAS, PRD) — agents must be aware of target system
SAP authorization objects are extremely granular — missing auth causes opaque '403' errors
On-premise SAP requires VPN/RFC connectivity — not directly internet-accessible
SAP business objects have system-specific configuration that affects what data is available
API Hub sandbox data is sanitized — behavior differs from real production SAP systems
